text: In which year did World War II begin?
explanation: World War II began on 1 September 1939 with Germany's invasion of Poland.

text: Who was the first human in space?
explanation: Yuri Gagarin made the first crewed spaceflight on 12 April 1961.

text: In which year did humans first land on the Moon?
explanation: The Apollo 11 crew landed on the Moon on 20 July 1969.

text: Which ancient civilization built the pyramids of Giza?
explanation: The Giza pyramids were built by the ancient Egyptians around 2500 BC.

text: Who was the first President of the United States?
explanation: George Washington became the first U.S. president in 1789.

text: In which year did the Soviet Union dissolve?
explanation: The Soviet Union officially ceased to exist in December 1991.

text: Who led Nazi Germany during World War II?
explanation: Adolf Hitler was the leader of Nazi Germany.

text: Which passenger liner sank in 1912 after hitting an iceberg?
explanation: The Titanic sank on the night of 15 April 1912 on its maiden voyage.

text: In which city is the ancient Colosseum located?
explanation: The Colosseum is an amphitheatre in Rome, built in the 1st century AD.

text: Who was the founder of Marxism and author of 'Das Kapital'?
explanation: Karl Marx wrote 'Das Kapital' and, with Engels, developed Marxism.

text: Which country did Queen Cleopatra rule?
explanation: Cleopatra VII was the last ruler of Hellenistic Egypt.

text: In which year did World War I begin?
explanation: World War I began in 1914 and lasted until 1918.

text: Who led the October Revolution of 1917 in Russia?
explanation: The Bolsheviks, led by Lenin, took power in the October Revolution of 1917.

text: What was the capital of the Byzantine Empire called?
explanation: The capital of Byzantium was Constantinople.

text: Who reached the Americas in 1492 by sailing west?
explanation: Christopher Columbus's expedition reached the Americas in 1492.

text: On which continent did the civilization of Ancient Egypt arise?
explanation: Ancient Egypt lay in north-eastern Africa along the Nile.

text: Who became the first Emperor of the French in 1804?
explanation: Napoleon Bonaparte crowned himself Emperor of the French in 1804.

text: Who was the first woman in space?
explanation: Valentina Tereshkova flew into space in 1963.

text: Which wall divided Germany's main city until 1989?
explanation: The Berlin Wall divided the city from 1961 to 1989.

text: Who was the last Russian emperor?
explanation: Nicholas II abdicated in 1917, the last Russian emperor.

text: In which year was the Battle of Kulikovo?
explanation: The Battle of Kulikovo between Dmitry Donskoy and Mamai took place in 1380.

text: Which prince converted Kievan Rus to Christianity in 988?
explanation: The Christianization of Rus took place under Prince Vladimir in 988.

text: Which battle is regarded as the turning point on the Eastern Front of WWII?
explanation: The Battle of Stalingrad (1942–1943) was the turning point of the war in the East.

text: In which year was the U.S. Declaration of Independence adopted?
explanation: The Declaration of Independence was adopted on 4 July 1776.

text: Which revolution began in France in 1789?
explanation: The French Revolution began in 1789 with the storming of the Bastille.

text: Who was the first ruler to be crowned Tsar of Russia?
explanation: Ivan IV (the Terrible) was crowned Tsar in 1547, the first Russian tsar.

text: In which year did the Western Roman Empire fall?
explanation: The Western Roman Empire fell in 476 when Romulus Augustulus was deposed.

text: Which event marked the start of the German–Soviet war (the 'Great Patriotic War')?
options: Germany's invasion of the USSR on 22 June 1941
explanation: It began on 22 June 1941 with Germany's invasion of the USSR.

text: Who was the British Prime Minister during World War II?
explanation: Winston Churchill led the British government from 1940 to 1945.

text: What was Mikhail Gorbachev's policy of reform in the late 1980s called?
explanation: Perestroika was the reform programme launched by Gorbachev in 1985.

text: Which dynasty ruled Russia from 1613 to 1917?
explanation: The Romanov dynasty ruled Russia for over 300 years, from 1613 to 1917.

text: To which historical era does Leonardo da Vinci belong?
explanation: Leonardo da Vinci was one of the greatest masters of the Renaissance.

text: Who led the French invasion of Russia in 1812?
explanation: The Patriotic War of 1812 was fought against Napoleon's army.

text: Which Roman city was destroyed by the eruption of Vesuvius in AD 79?
explanation: The AD 79 eruption of Vesuvius buried Pompeii and Herculaneum.

text: Who was the first President of Russia?
explanation: Boris Yeltsin was elected the first President of Russia in 1991.

text: In which year did the American Civil War begin?
explanation: The American Civil War lasted from 1861 to 1865.

text: Who unified Germany in 1871 through a policy of 'blood and iron'?
explanation: Chancellor Otto von Bismarck united the German states into one empire in 1871.

text: Which peace treaty formally ended World War I?
explanation: The Treaty of Versailles in 1919 concluded World War I.

text: Who commanded the assault on Berlin in 1945 as head of the 1st Belorussian Front?
explanation: The Berlin operation was led by Marshal Georgy Zhukov.

text: Which ancient South American civilization built Machu Picchu?
explanation: Machu Picchu was built by the Inca Empire in the 15th century.

text: In which year was the Battle of Poltava?
explanation: The Battle of Poltava, Peter I's defeat of the Swedes, took place in 1709.

text: Which 1215 document limited the power of the English king?
explanation: Magna Carta was sealed by King John in 1215.

text: Who set out the heliocentric system of the world in a 1543 work?
explanation: Copernicus published his heliocentric model in 1543, the year of his death.

text: In which year did the Decembrist revolt take place?
explanation: The Decembrist revolt on Senate Square occurred on 14 December 1825.

text: Which battle of 1066 led to the Norman conquest of England?
explanation: At Hastings in 1066, William the Conqueror defeated the Anglo-Saxons.

text: Who was the last pharaoh of Hellenistic Egypt before Roman rule?
explanation: Cleopatra VII was the last ruler of the Ptolemaic dynasty.

text: In which year was serfdom abolished in Russia?
explanation: Serfdom was abolished by Alexander II's manifesto in 1861.

text: What is the period of mass executions in the French Revolution, linked to Robespierre, called?
explanation: The Reign of Terror of 1793–1794 was led by Maximilien Robespierre.

text: Which battle of 480 BC made 300 Spartans famous?
explanation: At Thermopylae, King Leonidas and his Spartans held off the Persians in 480 BC.

text: Who started the Reformation by posting 95 theses in 1517?
explanation: Martin Luther posted his 95 theses in Wittenberg in 1517.

text: In which year did Napoleon suffer his final defeat at Waterloo?
explanation: The Battle of Waterloo took place in 1815 and ended Napoleon's era.

text: Which empire fell with the capture of Constantinople in 1453?
explanation: In 1453 the Ottomans took Constantinople, ending the Byzantine Empire.

text: Who was the first tsar of the Romanov dynasty?
explanation: Michael Romanov was elected tsar in 1613.

text: Which 1648 peace ended the Thirty Years' War?
explanation: The Peace of Westphalia in 1648 ended the Thirty Years' War.

text: In which year did the storming of the Bastille take place?
explanation: The Bastille fell on 14 July 1789, the symbolic start of the French Revolution.

text: Which Mongol ruler founded the largest empire of the 13th century?
explanation: Genghis Khan united the Mongol tribes and built a vast empire in the early 13th century.

text: Which Russian prince defeated the Swedes in the Battle of the Neva in 1240?
explanation: For his victory on the Neva in 1240, Prince Alexander earned the name Nevsky.

text: In which year was the United Nations founded?
explanation: The UN was founded in 1945 after World War II.

text: Which general led the Carthaginians and crossed the Alps with elephants in the Second Punic War?
explanation: Hannibal Barca crossed the Alps and invaded Italy in 218 BC.

text: Who discovered the sea route to India around Africa in 1498?
explanation: The Portuguese explorer Vasco da Gama reached India by sea around Africa in 1498.

text: What year was Canada founded in?
explanation: The correct answer is 1867.

text: What year did the Battle of Agincourt take place?
explanation: The correct answer is 1415.

text: What was the real name of the Albanian national leader Skanderbeg?
explanation: The correct answer is Gjergj Kastrioti.

text: What battle was the longest in World War I, lasting approximately 303 days?
explanation: The correct answer is Battle of Verdun.

text: Spain was formed in 1469 with the marriage of Isabella I of Castile and Ferdinand II of what other Iberian kingdom?
explanation: The correct answer is Aragon.

text: In the year 1900, what were the most popular first names given to boy and girl babies born in the United States?
explanation: The correct answer is John and Mary.

text: What year did World War I begin?
explanation: The correct answer is 1914.

text: What year did the Boxing Day earthquake & tsunami occur in the Indian Ocean?
explanation: The correct answer is 2004.

text: When did the United States formally declare war on Japan, entering World War II?
explanation: The correct answer is December 8, 1941.

text: On what street did the 1666 Great Fire of London start?

text: Which Irish village in County Mayo built a tourism industry on the back of an alleged appearance by the Virgin Mary in 1879?

text: Which country had an "Orange Revolution" between 2004 and 2005?

text: What was the aim of the "Umbrella Revolution" in Hong Kong in 2014?

text: Which Las Vegas casino was originally constructed and operated by mobster Bugsy Siegel?
explanation: The correct answer is The Flamingo.

text: Who was the first explorer to sail to North America?

text: Who was the last Roman emperor in the Year of Four Emperors (69 AD)?
explanation: The correct answer is Vespasian.

text: Joseph Smith was the founder of what religion?
explanation: The correct answer is Mormonism.

text: On what day did Germany invade Poland?

text: What number does the Roman numeral "D" stand for?
explanation: The correct answer is 500.

text: These two countries held a commonwealth from the 16th to 18th century.
explanation: The correct answer is Poland and Lithuania.

text: What was the first recorded object to ever reach space?

text: How many women joined the United States Armed Services during World War II?

text: The Second Boer War in 1899 was fought where?

text: In the War of the Pacific (1879 - 1883), Bolivia lost its access to the Pacific Ocean after being defeated by which South American country?
explanation: The correct answer is Chile.

explanation: The correct answer is Polio.

explanation: The correct answer is 1990.

text: When did the French Revolution begin?
explanation: The correct answer is 1789.

text: One of the deadliest pandemics, the "Spanish Flu", killed off what percentage of the human world population at the time?
explanation: The correct answer is 3 to 6 percent.

text: What was the official German currency until 2002?

text: When did the Byzantine Empire collapse?

text: Which of the following battles is often considered as marking the beginning of the fall of the Western Roman Empire?

text: What Russian automatic gas-operated assault rifle was developed in the Soviet Union in 1947, and is still popularly used today?
explanation: The correct answer is AK-47.

text: Which modern country is known as "The Graveyard of Empires"?

text: Which of the following years is commonly referred to as the "Year Without a Summer"?

text: In which ancient country did Pharaoh Tutankhamun rule, whose tomb was discovered intact?
explanation: Tutankhamun's tomb was opened by Howard Carter in 1922 in the Valley of the Kings.

text: Which famous general and dictator of Ancient Rome was assassinated by conspirators led by Brutus in the Ides of March?
explanation: Julius Caesar was assassinated on March 15, 44 BC in the Senate.

text: Which great 15th-century invention by Johannes Gutenberg revolutionized the spread of knowledge?
explanation: Gutenberg invented movable metal type printing around 1440.

text: In which year did the storming of the Bastille occur, marking the start of the French Revolution?
explanation: The Bastille was taken by French revolutionaries on July 14, 1789.

text: Which charter, signed by the English King John in 1215, limited the absolute power of the monarch?
explanation: Magna Carta laid the foundations for English constitutional law and civil rights.

text: Which country is considered the birthplace of the Industrial Revolution, which started in the late 18th century?
explanation: The Industrial Revolution began in England due to steam engines and textile machinery.

text: In which famous 1815 battle did Napoleon Bonaparte suffer his final and crushing defeat?
explanation: The defeat at Waterloo led to Napoleon's final abdication and exile to St. Helena.

text: Which peace treaty officially ended World War I in 1919?
explanation: The Treaty of Versailles was signed on June 28, 1919 in the Hall of Mirrors of the Palace of Versailles.

text: Which ancient code of laws, carved on a basalt stele, was compiled by the King of Babylon around 1750 BC?
explanation: The Code of Hammurabi contains 282 articles and utilizes the principle of talion ('an eye for an eye').

text: Which event in 1618 served as the direct trigger for the start of the devastating Thirty Years' War?
explanation: Protestant nobles threw Catholic imperial representatives out of the windows of Prague Castle.

text: Which reform era in Japan (1868-1912) restored power to the Emperor and modernized the country?
explanation: The Meiji Restoration opened Japan to the world and converted it into an industrial empire.

text: Which stone slab, discovered in Egypt in 1799, allowed Jean-Francois Champollion to decipher Egyptian hieroglyphs?
explanation: The slab contains the same decree in three scripts: hieroglyphic, Demotic, and Ancient Greek.

text: When did World War II begin?
explanation: Germany invaded Poland Sept 1, 1939.

explanation: Washington 1789–1797.

text: Which of the following Presidents of the United States was assassinated?
explanation: The correct answer is William McKinley.

text: In what year did Texas secede from Mexico?
explanation: The correct answer is 1836.

text: What did the abbreviation "RMS" stand for in the RMS Titanic in 1912?
explanation: The correct answer is Royal Mail Ship.

text: Which king was killed at the Battle of Bosworth Field in 1485?
explanation: The correct answer is Richard III.

text: Against which country did the Dutch Republic fight the Eighty Years' War?
explanation: The correct answer is Spain.

text: The coat of arms of the King of Spain contains the arms from the monarchs of Castille, Leon, Aragon and which other former Iberian kingdom?
explanation: The correct answer is Navarre.

text: Which German field marshal was known as the `Desert Fox`?
explanation: The correct answer is Erwin Rommel.

text: What was Manfred von Richthofen's nickname?
explanation: The correct answer is The Red Baron.

text: What is the bloodiest event in United States history, in terms of casualties?
explanation: The correct answer is Battle of Antietam.

text: What year is considered to be the year that the British Empire ended?
explanation: The correct answer is 1997.

text: During WWII, in 1945, the United States dropped atomic bombs on the two Japanese cities of Hiroshima and what other city?
explanation: The correct answer is Nagasaki.

text: How was Socrates executed?
explanation: The correct answer is Poison.

text: On which day did construction start on "The Pentagon", the headquarters for the United States Department of Defense?
explanation: The correct answer is September 11, 1941.

text: What was Genghis Khan's real name?
explanation: The correct answer is Temüjin.

text: The fraudelent doctor John R. Brinkley acummulated great fame and wealth in the early 1900s offering what service?
explanation: The correct answer is Goat testicles transplant.

text: When did Spanish Peninsular War start?
explanation: The correct answer is 1808.

text: Who was the Prime Minister of Japan when Japan declared war on the US?
explanation: The correct answer is Hideki Tojo.

text: Which U.S. President was famously 'attacked' by a swimming rabbit?
explanation: The correct answer is Jimmy Carter.

text: When Christopher Columbus sailed to America, what was the first region he arrived in?
explanation: The correct answer is The Bahamas Archipelago.

text: When did Norway become free from Sweden?
explanation: The correct answer is 1905.

text: How old was Adolf Hitler when he died?
explanation: The correct answer is 56.

text: Which country gifted the Statue of Liberty to the United States of America?
explanation: The correct answer is France.

text: Who was the leader of the Communist Party of Yugoslavia?
explanation: The correct answer is Josip Broz Tito.

text: When did the Crisis of the Third Century begin?
explanation: The correct answer is 235 AD.

text: During the Mongolian invasions of Japan, what were the Mongol boats mostly stopped by?
explanation: The correct answer is Typhoons.

text: When did the Battle of the Bulge end?
explanation: The correct answer is January 25, 1945.

text: What was the capital of South Vietnam before the Vietnam War?
explanation: The correct answer is Saigon.

text: How many manned moon landings have there been?
explanation: The correct answer is 6.

text: What event marked the start of World War II?
explanation: The correct answer is Invasion of Poland (1939).

text: Before the American colonies switched to the Gregorian calendar in 1752, on what date did their new year start?
explanation: The correct answer is March 25th.

text: The collapse of the Soviet Union took place in which year?
explanation: The correct answer is 1991.

text: Which famous world leader is famed for the saying, "Let them eat cake", yet is rumored that he/she never said it at all?
explanation: The correct answer is Marie Antoinette.

text: How many sonatas did Ludwig van Beethoven write?
explanation: The correct answer is 32.

text: What was the name of the spy ring that helped the United States win the Revolutionary War?
explanation: The correct answer is Culper Ring.

text: In what year did Kentucky become the 15th state to join the union?
explanation: The correct answer is 1792.

text: When did Canada leave the confederation to become their own nation?
explanation: The correct answer is July 1st, 1867.

text: In 1961, an American B-52 aircraft crashed and nearly detonated two 4mt nuclear bombs over which US city?
explanation: The correct answer is Goldsboro, North Carolina.

text: Which of the following ancient Near Eastern peoples still exists as a modern ethnic group?
explanation: The correct answer is Assyrians.

text: In 1845, a series of wars named after which indigenous people began in New Zealand?
explanation: The correct answer is Māori.

text: John Moses Browning, the designer of the M1918 BAR (Browning Automatic Rifle) was a part of which religion?
explanation: The correct answer is Mormon.

text: The Bohemian Revolt (1618-1620) started after Protestants in Prague did what to their Catholic Lords Regents?
options: Threw them out of a window
explanation: The correct answer is Threw them out of a window.

text: What did the first vending machines in the early 1880's dispense?
explanation: The correct answer is Post cards.

text: Which U.S. president was said to have been too honest to lie to his father about chopping down a cherry tree?
explanation: The correct answer is George Washington.

text: In what prison was Adolf Hitler held in 1924?
explanation: The correct answer is Landsberg Prison.

text: On which aircraft carrier did the Doolitte Raid launch from on April 18, 1942 during World War II?
explanation: The correct answer is USS Hornet.

text: Which of the following countries was not an axis power during World War II?
explanation: The correct answer is Soviet Union.

text: During what war did the "Cuban Missile Crisis" occur?
explanation: The correct answer is Cold War.

text: When was the city of Rome, Italy founded?
explanation: The correct answer is 753 BCE.

text: King Henry VIII was the second monarch of which European royal house?
explanation: The correct answer is Tudor.

text: Which Apollo mission was the last one in NASA's Apollo program?
explanation: The correct answer is Apollo 17.

text: Who was among those killed in the 2010 Smolensk, Russia plane crash tragedy?
explanation: The correct answer is The Polish President.

text: List the following Iranic empires in chronological order:
explanation: The correct answer is Median, Achaemenid, Parthian, Sassanid.

text: The main objective of the German operation "Case Blue" during World War II was originally to capture what?
explanation: The correct answer is Caucasus.

text: Which of the following Physicists aided Nazi Germany in their production of a nuclear weapon?
explanation: The correct answer is Werner Heisenberg.

text: When did Lithuania declare independence from the Soviet Union?
explanation: The correct answer is March 11th, 1990.

text: During World War I, which nation's monarchs were blood related?
explanation: The correct answer is England, Germany, Russia.

text: What historical event was Tchaikovsky's 1812 Overture referencing?
options: The Charge of the Light Brigade (Crimean War)
explanation: The correct answer is The Napoleonic Wars.

text: What was Napoleon Bonaparte's name before he changed it?
explanation: The correct answer is Napoleone di Buonaparte.

text: How old was Lyndon B. Johnson when he assumed the role of President of the United States?
explanation: The correct answer is 55.

text: In relation to the British Occupation in Ireland, what does the IRA stand for.
explanation: The correct answer is Irish Republican Army.

text: The pantheon in Rome was used to worship what god?
explanation: The correct answer is Any god they wanted.

text: When was Adolf Hitler appointed as Chancellor of Germany?
explanation: The correct answer is January 30, 1933.

text: All of the following are names of the Seven Warring States EXCEPT:
explanation: The correct answer is Zhai (翟).

text: Which country did the Eureka Rebellion, an 1856 battle against colonial rule, take place in?
explanation: The correct answer is Australia.

text: How many years did the Hundred Years' War last?
explanation: The correct answer is 116.

text: According to scholarly estimates, what percentage of the world population at the time died due to Tamerlane's conquests?
explanation: The correct answer is 5%.

text: What year did Australia become a federation?
explanation: The correct answer is 1901.

text: The ancient city of Chichén Itzá was built by which civilization?
explanation: The correct answer is Mayans.

text: What was the transfer of disease, crops, and people across the Atlantic shortly after the discovery of the Americas called?
explanation: The correct answer is The Columbian Exchange.

text: Which Nation DID NOT have a Colony in Modern-day America?
explanation: The correct answer is Portugal.

text: Which of the following snipers has the highest amount of confirmed kills?
explanation: The correct answer is Simo Häyhä.

text: Which of these positions did the astronomer and physicist Isaac Newton not hold?
options: Surveyor to the City of London
explanation: The correct answer is Surveyor to the City of London.

text: When did the British hand-over sovereignty of Hong Kong back to China?
explanation: The correct answer is 1997.

text: In what year was the video game company Electronic Arts founded?
explanation: The correct answer is 1982.

text: Which countries participated in the Lobster War?
explanation: The correct answer is France and Brazil.

text: Who was the only US President to be elected four times?
explanation: The correct answer is Franklin Roosevelt.

text: What was the name commonly given to the ancient trade routes that connected the East and West of Eurasia?
explanation: The correct answer is Silk Road.

text: What year did the Vietnam War end?
explanation: The correct answer is 1975.

text: What is the mnemonic device for remembering the fates of the wives of Henry VIII?
options: Divorced, Beheaded, Died, Divorced, Beheaded, Survived
Beheaded, Died, Divorced, Divorced, Beheaded, Survived
Died, Beheaded, Divorced, Beheaded, Survived, Divorced
Survived, Beheaded, Died, Divorced, Divorced, Beheaded
explanation: The correct answer is Divorced, Beheaded, Died, Divorced, Beheaded, Survived.

text: Who was the first prime minister of Canada?
explanation: The correct answer is John Macdonald.

text: Which King of England was faced with the Peasants' Revolt in 1381?
explanation: The correct answer is Richard II.

text: Which one of these was not a beach landing site in the Invasion of Normandy?
explanation: The correct answer is Silver.

text: Who was the first American in space?
explanation: The correct answer is Alan Shephard.

text: In what year did the Berlin Wall fall?
explanation: The correct answer is 1989.

text: Adolf Hitler was born on which date?
explanation: The correct answer is April 20, 1889.

text: Which one of these rulers did not belong to the Habsburg dynasty?
explanation: The correct answer is Philip V.

text: Which of these 1900s tanks were designed and built BEFORE the others?
explanation: The correct answer is Renault FT.

text: Joseph Stalin had a criminal past doing what?
explanation: The correct answer is Robbing Trains.

text: What was found in 1946 by two young shepards near a cave?
explanation: The correct answer is Dead Sea Scrolls.

text: In what year did the Great Northern War, between Russia and Sweden, end?
explanation: The correct answer is 1721.

text: In 1720, England was in massive debt and became involved in the South Sea Bubble. Who was the main mastermind behind it?
explanation: The correct answer is John Blunt.

text: What was the first sport to have been played on the moon?
explanation: The correct answer is Golf.

text: Which U.S. president took part in the Potsdam Conference, where the Allies reached a peace settlement with Germany?
explanation: The correct answer is Harry S. Truman.

text: When was "The Gadget", the first nuclear device to be detonated, tested?
explanation: The correct answer is July 16, 1945.

text: When was the SS or Schutzstaffel established?
explanation: The correct answer is April 4th, 1925.

text: Who tutored Alexander the Great?
explanation: The correct answer is Aristotle.

text: What was the total length of the Titanic?
explanation: The correct answer is 882 ft | 268.8 m.

text: Which building was set aflame on August 24th, 1812?
explanation: The correct answer is The White House.

text: Who assassinated President James A. Garfield?
explanation: The correct answer is Charles Guiteau.

text: What does the United States of America celebrate during the 4th of July?
options: The signing of the Declaration of Independence
The anniversary of the Battle of Gettysburg
The crossing of the Delaware River
explanation: The correct answer is The signing of the Declaration of Independence.

text: Who is attributed credit for recording the epic poem The Odyssey?
explanation: The correct answer is Homer.

text: Which country was Josef Stalin born in?
explanation: The correct answer is Georgia.

text: Which of the following is not in the Indo-European language family?
explanation: The correct answer is Finnish.

text: The Tsar Bomba, the most powerful nuclear bomb ever tested, had a yield of 50 megatons but theoretically had a maximum yield of how much?
explanation: The correct answer is 100 Megatons.

text: The original Roman alphabet lacked the following letters EXCEPT:
explanation: The correct answer is X.

text: The seed drill was invented by which British inventor?
explanation: The correct answer is Jethro Tull.

text: Which English king was married to Eleanor of Aquitaine?
explanation: The correct answer is Henry II.

text: Abolitionist John Brown raided the arsenal in which Virginia Town?
explanation: The correct answer is Harper's Ferry.

text: With which Greek philosopher would you associate the phrase, "I know that I know nothing"?
explanation: The correct answer is Socrates.

text: What were the first states to break away from Yugoslavia?
explanation: The correct answer is Slovenia, Croatia.

text: Which of the following was Brazil was a former colony under?
explanation: The correct answer is Portugal.

text: In 1453, which important city fell?
explanation: The correct answer is Constantinople.
